markets.question: QatarEnergy announces/resumes LNG production in Qatar by May 31?
markets.description:
QatarEnergy, a Qatari state-owned petroleum company, announced on March 2 that it would halt production of liquefied natural gas (LNG) due to military strikes on its operating facilities (see: https://www.qatarenergy.qa/en/MediaCenter/Pages/newsdetails.aspx?ItemId=3892).

This market will resolve to “Yes” if QatarEnergy resumes production of liquefied natural gas at QatarEnergy LNG production facilities in Qatar, or officially announces that such production has resumed or will resume, by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

QatarEnergy resuming the production of other halted products, including downstream LNG-related products, or resuming transportation of LNG without resuming production, will not alone count.

An official announcement that LNG production will resume at QatarEnergy LNG production facilities in Qatar must signal the end of the total LNG production halt effective immediately or on a specific date or clearly defined time window. Mere statements that production will resume at some undefined point in the future, or that production will resume once the halt has ended, will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from QatarEnergy (https://www.qatarenergy.qa/en/Pages/vHome.aspx); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 42.5 in Game 1?
markets.description:
In the upcoming Lower bracket semifinal match between Power Rangers and South America Rejects in the DreamLeague Division 2 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 30 at 8:30AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 43 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

events.title: Dota 2: Power Rangers vs South America Rejects (BO3) - DreamLeague Division 2 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Lower bracket semifinal match between Power Rangers and South America Rejects in the DreamLeague Division 2 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 30 at 8:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Power Rangers" if Power Rangers win the match against South America Rejects.

This market will resolve to "South America Rejects" if South America Rejects win the match against Power Rangers.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: South America Rejects hold the edge entering this DreamLeague Division 2 Season 4 lower bracket semifinal BO3 rematch against Power Rangers, following their 2-0 group stage sweep on April 21 and Group B-topping 6-1 record (12-4 maps). SAR's momentum peaks with four consecutive lower bracket victories, capped by a dominant 2-0 over second-place Zero Tenacity on April 29, leveraging strong drafts around Wits and DarkMago♡ amid 59% three-month winrate. Power Rangers, third in group at 5-2 (11-6 maps), showed resilience post-upper bracket semifinal loss to 1w Team, rallying with a 2-1 lower quarterfinal win over L1GA TEAM on April 28 (61% winrate). Winner faces 1w Team in lower final for DreamLeague S29 qualification path in double-elimination playoffs.

markets.question: CJ McCollum: Points O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 30 at 12:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if CJ McCollum scores more than 4.5 points during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if CJ McCollum scores 4.5 points or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

events.title: Knicks vs. Hawks
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 30 at 12:00AM ET:
If the Knicks win, the market will resolve to "Knicks".
If the Hawks win, the market will resolve to "Hawks".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold a 3-2 series lead over the Atlanta Hawks in their Eastern Conference first-round NBA playoffs matchup, fueled by a dominant 126-97 Game 5 blowout at Madison Square Garden where Jalen Brunson erupted for 39 points amid strong team defense and rebounding. Heading into critical Game 6 tonight in Atlanta, trader consensus reflects Knicks' momentum from recent form, contrasted by the Hawks' must-win desperation on home court with crowd support. Key injury concerns include Knicks guard Josh Hart (questionable, lower back contusion) and Hawks center Jock Landale (out, ankle), alongside Keshon Gilbert (out, abdomen); no other major Knicks issues reported after Brunson's Game 4 ankle scare. Hawks seek to extend via Trae Young's scoring and improved physicality, while Knicks leverage rest advantages and playoff experience.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both New Meta and Arneb each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both New Meta and Arneb each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both New Meta and Arneb. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 1, or if Game 1 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both New Meta and Arneb each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 2. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 56.5 in Game 1?
markets.description:
In the upcoming Lower bracket semifinal match between Power Rangers and South America Rejects in the DreamLeague Division 2 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 30 at 8:30AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 57 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lucknow be 37°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/in/lucknow/VILK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran negotiations for a permanent peace deal remain stalled following the collapse of indirect talks in Pakistan earlier this month, with a fragile ceasefire—indefinitely extended by President Trump on April 21—holding amid mutual blockades on the Strait of Hormuz and Iranian ports. Iran's April 27 proposal offers to reopen the strait and end hostilities in exchange for lifting the US naval blockade, while deferring nuclear program curbs, sanctions relief, and ballistic missile limits—core US demands that Secretary Rubio called unresolved sticking points. Iran's foreign minister blamed Washington during April 27 Russia talks, as Germany accused Tehran of stalling. Upcoming mediation rounds could shift dynamics, but entrenched disputes sustain trader skepticism on near-term resolution.

markets.question: X banned in any European country by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the use of X/Twitter is banned within any European country by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a “European country” is defined as any of the following sovereign states: Albania, Andorra, Austria, Belarus, Belgium, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czechia, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Georgia, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Ireland, Italy, Kosovo, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Moldova, Monaco, Montenegro, Netherlands, North Macedonia, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, San Marino, Serbia, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Turkey, Ukraine, United Kingdom, and Vatican City.

A ban will qualify if legislation is enacted or government action is taken to bar the respective country's citizens from downloading and/or viewing X/Twitter, and/or posting on X/Twitter. Any legislation or government action that meets these standards will qualify, regardless of whether or when the ban goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the respective government and X/Twitter; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 67% implied probability for no ban of X in any European country by year-end, driven by the EU's preference for hefty fines over drastic platform bans under the Digital Services Act (DSA). In December 2025, the European Commission imposed a €120 million penalty on X for transparency lapses in blue-check verification, ad libraries, and researcher data access—escalating tensions but stopping short of suspension, as regulators prioritize compliance fixes and revenue extraction from major platforms. X appealed the fine in February 2026 at the General Court, signaling prolonged legal battles amid retaliatory moves like blocking EU ad accounts. No national bans have materialized despite prior threats, underscoring DSA enforcement's focus on policy tweaks rather than outright prohibitions, with court rulings as the next key catalyst.

markets.question: Will Ted Cruz post 140-159 posts from April 28 to May 5, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Ted Cruz (@tedcruz), posts on X between April 28, 12:00 PM ET and May 5, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Hong Kong have 190mm or more of precipitation in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in mm in Hong Kong between April 1 and April 30, 2026, according to the Hong Kong Observatory.

The resolution source for this market will be the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Total Rainfall (mm)" figure for all days in April 2026 under the "Daily Extract" at the https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm link once figures are finalized for the specified range.

If the reported data falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

If the relevant data is not available by June 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 1 decimal place (e.g., 1.5), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Mexico GDP growth in Q1 2026 be between 0.5% and 1.0%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to Mexico's Y/Y Growth Rate of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in the "Timely Estimate of Quarterly GDP" release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for release on April 30, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://en.www.inegi.org.mx/app/saladeprensa/

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

markets.question: Israel x Hamas ceasefire cancelled by June 30?

eventMetadata.context_description: A fragile US-brokered Gaza ceasefire, effective since October 2025, faces mounting strain six months in, with trader consensus reflecting mutual accusations of violations amid stalled phase-two talks on Hamas disarmament and Israeli withdrawal. Hamas rejected a multinational peace board's April 14 disarmament mandate, insisting Israel first fully implement phase-one aid and reconstruction commitments, while IDF reports over 30 Palestinian terrorist breaches since early April, including attempted rearmament. Israeli strikes have killed at least 700-800 Palestinians per local health officials, prompting UN warnings of fragility. Upcoming direct US-Hamas negotiations and aid flows through Kerem Shalom could tip escalation risks, as both sides leverage the truce for military repositioning without formal cancellation.

markets.question: Set 1 Winner: Baptiste vs Andreeva
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Hailey Baptiste and Mirra Andreeva in the Madrid Open, originally scheduled for April 30, 2026 at 10:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to “Baptiste” if Hailey Baptiste wins the first set. It will resolve to “Andreeva” if Mirra Andreeva wins the first set.
If the match begins but is not completed, and the first set is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on that completed set. If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA match statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Madrid Open: Hailey Baptiste vs Mirra Andreeva Set 1 Winner

events.title: Madrid Open: Hailey Baptiste vs Mirra Andreeva
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Hailey Baptiste and Mirra Andreeva in the Madrid Open, originally scheduled for April 30, 2026 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Hailey Baptiste' if Hailey Baptiste advances against Mirra Andreeva.

This market will resolve to 'Mirra Andreeva' if Mirra Andreeva advances against Hailey Baptiste.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hailey Baptiste's stunning quarterfinal upset over world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka—saving six match points in a dramatic tiebreak—has propelled the No. 32-ranked American into her first WTA 1000 semifinal on Madrid's outdoor clay, marking her deepest run at the event amid a string of seeded scalps. No. 8 seed Mirra Andreeva, seeking her first Madrid final, advanced steadily by saving three set points against Leylah Fernandez and grinding out a 2:53 marathon win over Anna Bondar earlier in the week. Andreeva leads their head-to-head 1-0 from Wimbledon grass last year, but Baptiste's clay momentum challenges the Russian's superior ranking and consistent deep runs here; no injuries reported ahead of Thursday's clash at La Caja Magica.

markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the VA-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 16, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former U.S. Rep. Elaine Luria leads Polymarket odds at 70.5% implied probability for the VA-02 Democratic primary on August 4, 2026, driven by her prior representation of the district from 2019-2023, superior name recognition among voters in Virginia Beach and the Eastern Shore, and dominant fundraising with nearly $2.9 million raised and $2.3 million cash on hand per March 31 filings—far outpacing rivals like James Osyf ($411,000 raised) and Nila Devanath ($230,000). Key endorsements from Sens. Tim Kaine and Mark Warner, Gov.-elect Abigail Spanberger, EMILYs List, and DCCC's Red to Blue targeting bolster her position in the fragmented seven-candidate field. Recent Q1 reports and campaign events underscore trader consensus on her frontrunner status, though a crowded primary could split anti-Luria votes.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $6,200 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold futures for June 2026 delivery (GC) hover near $4,650 per ounce, reflecting trader caution amid sticky inflation—March CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year—and a hawkish Federal Reserve stance, with policymakers projecting just one rate cut in 2026 following their April meeting hold. Elevated oil prices from the protracted Iran conflict fuel inflation fears and a stronger U.S. dollar, pressuring non-yielding gold, though central banks' robust buying (around 60 tonnes monthly) provides structural demand support. Recent daily gains stem from dollar softening, but sentiment hinges on May's CPI, nonfarm payrolls, PPI, and retail sales data ahead of the June FOMC, which could recalibrate rate path expectations and gold's end-June positioning.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Rep. Kevin Hern at 89% implied probability to win Oklahoma's Republican U.S. Senate primary on June 16, driven by his early March 11 announcement for the seat vacated by Sen. Markwayne Mullin's confirmation as DHS Secretary, followed by swift endorsements from President Trump, Senate Majority Leader John Thune, NRSC Chair Tim Scott, and Club for Growth. These backings, alongside Hern's House incumbency, fundraising edge, and conservative record, have consolidated support in the deep-red state, positioning him as the clear frontrunner. Former Attorney General John M. O’Connor at 9.8% and Lt. Gov. Matt Pinnell at 2.5% represent main challengers after the April filing deadline finalized a fragmented field, though late momentum or debates could narrow the gap absent polls.

markets.question: Will Google (GOOGL) close at $335-$340 on the final day of trading of the week of Apr 27 – May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Google (GOOGL) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final session of the week is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Google (GOOGL) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/GOOGL/history, published under "Historical Prices."

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 86.5% implied probability to Alphabet (GOOGL) closing the week of April 27 above $360, propelled by a blowout Q1 2026 earnings release on April 29 that delivered record revenue of $109.9 billion—up 22% year-over-year—and an 81% surge in net income to $37.7 billion, fueled by 63% Google Cloud growth and accelerating AI-driven demand in Search and YouTube advertising. Shares hit a 52-week high of $355.79 intraday on April 29 before closing at $349.94, with April 30 trading pushing highs near $377 amid sustained post-earnings momentum and analyst price targets averaging $382. Lower buckets reflect minimal downside risk given robust fundamentals and hyperscaler capital expenditure trends projected at $725 billion for 2026, though Friday's close remains the key resolution catalyst.

markets.question: Will the Ethiopian Citizens for Social Justice (EZEMA) win the most seats in the 2026 Ethiopian general election?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Ethiopia on June 1, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Ethiopian House of Peoples' Representatives election. 

If the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Ethiopian Parliament.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Ethiopian government, specifically the National Election Board of Ethiopia (NEBE) (nebe.org.et/en).

eventMetadata.context_description: Prosperity Party's 95% trader consensus for winning Ethiopia's parliamentary election reflects its entrenched institutional control as the incumbent under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, bolstered by a fragmented opposition and strategic non-contestation of all Tigray seats—likely ceded to TPLF—and dozens of Amhara constituencies to NaMA and others amid ongoing regional conflicts. Late March announcements confirmed this approach, allowing focus on sweeping victories elsewhere in the 547-seat House of Peoples' Representatives, echoing its 2021 landslide. National Election Board of Ethiopia's April voter education drives signal smooth June 1 preparations. Challenges would require unlikely late disruptions like widespread violence, unified opposition coalitions, or scandals derailing the process.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's April 2026 threats to withdraw the United States from NATO—triggered by European allies' refusal to dispatch ships amid escalating Strait of Hormuz tensions and the US-Israel conflict with Iran—have spurred trader discussion, though no formal notice has been issued under NATO Treaty Article 13, which requires one year. A 2023 National Defense Authorization Act bars unilateral presidential exit without two-thirds Senate approval or congressional legislation, facing strong bipartisan opposition in Congress. Recent White House comments, including from spokeswoman Caroline Leavitt ahead of a meeting with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, amplified rhetoric but yielded no action. Amid alliance debates on ending annual summits and hiking defense spending to 5% of GDP by 2035, trader consensus views structural barriers as outweighing short-term foreign policy pressures.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Toronto be 10°C on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ca/mississauga/CYYZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Environment Canada's latest forecast for Toronto Pearson International Airport projects a daytime high of 12°C on May 1 under increasing cloudiness with northwest winds at 20 km/h and a 30% chance of afternoon showers, yet trader consensus tilts toward 10°C (35% implied probability), closely trailed by 9°C (27%) and 11°C (22%), due to ensemble model spreads showing greater cooling from persistent cloud decks and cool-air advection. Recent overnight lows near 0°C with patchy frost have reinforced a chilly spring pattern below seasonal norms of 16°C, while diurnal heating potential hinges on afternoon sunshine breaks versus early shower development. New 12z GFS and ECMWF runs, plus morning observations, could refine these closely matched outcomes before resolution.

markets.question: Will West Ham be relegated from the English Premier League after the 2025–26 season?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs are relegated from the English Premier League after the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club is officially relegated by the English Premier League following the 2025–26 season, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Wolverhampton Wanderers and Burnley already mathematically relegated to the Championship, trader consensus centers on Tottenham Hotspur and West Ham United for the final Premier League drop spot, separated by just two points after 34 matches—Spurs on 34 amid their first top-flight win of 2026 via a late Joao Palhinha strike against Wolves, and West Ham on 36 thanks to Callum Wilson's stoppage-time goal versus Everton. Nottingham Forest (39 points) and Leeds United (40) have pulled five and six points clear respectively, bolstered by Forest's 5-0 thrashing of Sunderland and Leeds' Old Trafford upset, though neither is safe. Decisive run-ins loom, including Tottenham hosting Leeds on May 9 and West Ham entertaining Leeds on the final day May 24, with goal difference and head-to-head results pivotal amid varying fixture difficulties.

markets.question: Will Bitcoin reach $1,000,000 by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) between November 24, 2025, 14:00 and December 31, 2026, 23:59 in the ET timezone has a final "High" price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The India Meteorological Department (IMD)'s latest forecast dated April 30 positions Lucknow's maximum temperature on May 2 at 36°C under mainly clear skies with minimums around 24°C, driving trader consensus toward 36°C or below at 63.5% implied probability. This reflects cooling from April 29's observed 39.3°C peak amid partly cloudy conditions and isolated rain/thunderstorms earlier in the week, which increased cloud cover and suppressed daytime heating via reduced solar insolation. Seasonal norms for early May hover near 40°C, but current upper-air patterns favor moderation; however, model ensembles like GFS show slight divergence, with potential for 1-2°C upside if winds shift drier. Watch IMD's daily bulletin tomorrow for refined guidance ahead of resolution.

markets.question: Will Russia capture all of Pokrovsk by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the entirety of Pokrovsk, Donetsk Oblast, (48.303080° N, 37.192568° E) by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET.

Pokrovsk will be considered captured if the entirety of the municipality is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by the resolution date, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of the municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities with a darker grey line. If all area within the municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this will still qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Once Russia captures the entirety of the specified area, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/rwDQehLsZAUsquVX7

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have pressed toward Pokrovsk, a critical Donetsk logistics hub, advancing within 4-6 km of the city center as of late April 2026, but Ukrainian defenses—bolstered by drone strikes, artillery, and counteradvances east of Molodetske—have inflicted heavy attrition, repelling assaults near Hryshyne and Novooleksandrivka. Zelensky stated on April 10 that Moscow aims to seize Pokrovsk and nearby towns by month's end, yet Institute for the Study of War assessments through April 27 show no confirmed breakthroughs amid Russian unit fatigue post earlier gains. Trader consensus reflects this grinding stalemate, with open terrain favoring Ukrainian FPV drones; spring weather could enable maneuver, while manpower shortages and strikes on Russian rear infrastructure loom as pivotal risks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 29°C (30%) and 28°C (29%) for Shenzhen's highest temperature on May 2, reflecting forecast model variability amid persistent cloud cover and showers. Major services like AccuWeather and Weather Channel project highs of 28–28.3°C under partly cloudy to overcast conditions with 18–59% precipitation chances, while timeanddate.com leans cooler at 26°C due to expected passing showers suppressing daytime heating. Recent April days featured overcast skies and highs near 25–26°C, consistent with Shenzhen's humid subtropical climate where sea breezes and urban heat island effects modulate peaks around early May climatological averages of 28–29°C. New ECMWF and GFS runs expected within 24–48 hours could refine odds as resolution nears via official Shenzhen station measurements.

markets.question: Will Russia enter Svitle by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Svitle, Donetsk Oblast, (48.403602° N, 37.117327° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces continue persistent assaults in the Pokrovsk direction of Donetsk Oblast but have made no confirmed advances into Svitle—a small village north of Pokrovsk—according to the latest ISW maps as of April 29, 2026, anchoring trader consensus on low near-term entry probabilities. Geolocated footage shows Ukrainian counteradvances north of Pokrovsk, with Russian units redeploying from other sectors to offset losses from intense fighting and Ukrainian drone strikes on command posts. This grinding stalemate reflects broader challenges in Russia's spring offensive against Ukraine's Donetsk fortress belt, where logistical strains and unmanned systems disrupt momentum. Upcoming frontline escalations around Hryshyne and Rodynske could pressure Ukrainian defenses, though reinforcements sustain resistance; markets resolve per persistent ISW shading of Russian control in Svitle.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 27% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination due to his proactive national positioning, including a February book tour in New Hampshire that fueled speculation and a March California primary poll showing him trouncing Kamala Harris by 14 points at home. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 8.5% benefits from strong progressive appeal and youth poll strength, while Harris at 8.4% gains from her April tease of another run amid some national surveys like McLaughlin's showing her at 29%, though her 2024 defeat tempers enthusiasm. Jon Ossoff's 6.6% reflects Georgia incumbency gains. The wide-open field post-2024 loss hinges on 2026 midterm results, early-state fundraising, and endorsements, with no incumbent or clear frontrunner consolidating support yet.

markets.question: Will Discord’s market cap be $30B or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Discord's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by June 30, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Discord’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 77% implied probability against a Discord IPO by June 30, 2026, driven by the absence of a public S-1 filing following the company's confidential SEC submission in early January 2026 and banker hires from Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan for a targeted Q1 debut. Missed March timelines, amid volatile tech IPO markets and secondary share valuations dipping to $7–10 billion from 2021's $15 billion peak, have solidified skepticism on timing and scale. Conditional on listing, <15B market cap leads at 20.1%, reflecting revenue growth pressures and monetization hurdles in the competitive messaging sector; higher brackets trail below 3% each. Key catalyst: imminent S-1 disclosure or further delay signals.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Dog" in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

His survival of an apparent assassination attempt at the White House Correspondents' Dinner on April 25—followed by suspect charges on April 27—has heightened focus on resilient themes like Mar-a-Lago references from his campaign habits. markets.question: Spread: FC Barcelona (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for May 3 at 10:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Barcelona" if FC Barcelona win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Bayern München".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Barcelona vs. FC Bayern München - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for May 3 at 10:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona Femení hold a commanding position in Liga F with a near-perfect 24-0-1 record, propelled by recent thumpings of Real Madrid including a 3-0 quarter-final win on March 29 to secure their UEFA Women's Champions League semi-final spot against Bayern Frauen. Bayern, unbeaten domestically this season atop the Frauen-Bundesliga save for a 7-1 group-stage drubbing by Barcelona in October 2025, advanced via a gritty 5-3 aggregate over Manchester City with late strikes from Viggósdóttir and Dallmann. First leg on April 25 in Munich favors Bayern's home crowd and revenge motive, but Barcelona's depth shines despite Aitana Bonmatí's ongoing fibula absence until early May; Bayern miss winger Klara Bühl (muscle injury). Head-to-head favors Barça (4-1-0), underscoring trader focus on aggregate dynamics, clean sheets, and Salma Paralluelo's form.

Kane's recent form, including braces against Dortmund and consistent output post-February purple patch, underscores Bayern's attacking dominance amid favorable remaining fixtures with four matchdays left. Challengers like Luis Díaz and Serhou Guirassy (15 goals each) lack the volume to close the gap barring an unprecedented Kane injury sidelining him entirely while rivals notch multiple hat-tricks, an improbable scenario given his fitness recovery from early-April ankle issue.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy enters the Exort Series #25 Main Stage BO3 opener against Wave Esports as the stronger contender, bolstered by Team Spirit's academy pedigree and a revamped roster featuring VILBy, mazay, kidofpain, and s1nside since February 2026, yielding consistent Swiss stage advances like their 3-0 run here en route to grand final. Wave, HLTV #149 with a March refresh adding Katalic and sheezy, showed mixed form in qualifiers such as Urban Riga Open losses but upsets like over BIG Academy. Key factors include Spirit Academy's aggressive T-side prowess on meta maps like Dust2 and Mirage, rest advantage post-quals, and roster stability versus Wave's integration risks in this 16-team Swiss feeding playoffs.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 240-259 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Delfino Pescara 1936 win on 2026-05-01?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 1, 2026

events.title: Calcio Padova vs. Delfino Pescara 1936
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Serie B game, scheduled for Friday, May 1, 2026 between Calcio Padova and Delfino Pescara 1936.

eventMetadata.context_description: In a high-stakes Serie B relegation scrap, trader consensus slightly tilts toward Delfino Pescara (39.5%) over home side Calcio Padova (35.5%) ahead of their Stadio Euganeo showdown, driven by Pescara's potent attack (50 goals in 36 games) contrasting Padova's meager 34 goals and recent scoring slump (0.8 per game over last 10). Padova sit 14th on 40 points, six clear of 17th-placed Pescara (34 points), but host recent losses—including a 0-1 defeat to Virtus Entella—and absences of forwards Papu Gomez (ankle) and Kevin Lasagna (muscle) erode their edge. Pescara's mixed away form (2 wins in last 10) and defensive frailties (64 conceded) fuel the draw's 26.5% viability, echoing Padova's 1-0 head-to-head win at Pescara earlier this season.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Shenzhen be 29°C on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shenzhen/ZGSZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest AccuWeather and ensemble forecast models, including implied GFS and ECMWF guidance as of April 30, project Shenzhen's highest temperature on May 1 at around 27°C, with considerable cloudiness, 25% rain chance, and easterly winds moderating highs amid high humidity near 80%. This positions 27°C as the trader consensus at 38.5% implied probability, reflecting recent April variability—peaks near 30°C mid-month followed by cooler late-period lows around 17°C—and early May climatology averaging 28–29°C highs in the subtropical monsoon regime. Light precipitation risks and sea breeze influences suppress extremes above 29°C (10.5%), while baselines rule out sub-25°C outcomes. Updated model runs today and official Shenzhen Bao'an Airport observations will sharpen resolution.

markets.question: Valorant: FURIA Esports vs Sentinels (BO3) - VCT Americas Stage 1 Group Omega
markets.description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between FURIA Esports and Sentinels in the VCT Americas Stage 1 Group Omega, initially scheduled for May 2 at 8:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "FURIA Esports" if FURIA Esports win the match against Sentinels.

This market will resolve to "Sentinels" if Sentinels win the match against FURIA Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In VCT Americas Stage 1 Group Omega, Sentinels and FURIA enter their pivotal BO3 matchup both at 2-1 records, with Sentinels holding a slight edge on map differential (4-2 vs. FURIA's 4-3) as top-four playoff spots remain contested behind undefeated KRÜ. Sentinels built momentum with back-to-back 2-0 sweeps over 100 Thieves (Apr 19) and Evil Geniuses (Apr 26), boosting their rating, while FURIA rebounded from a 0-2 loss to KRÜ (Apr 25) after 2-1 and 2-0 wins versus Evil Geniuses and NRG. FURIA previously swept Sentinels 2-0 at Americas Kickoff, but both teams' rebuilt rosters introduce uncertainty amid the current meta and standard map pool. A win cements advancement for either side.

markets.question: Will Ted Cruz post 80-99 posts from April 24 to May 1, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Ted Cruz (@tedcruz), posts on X between April 24, 12:00 PM ET and May 1, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will there be a 1v5 ace clutch in the BLAST Rivals Fort Worth 2026 playoffs?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player completes a 1v5 ace clutch during the playoffs stage of BLAST Rivals Fort Worth 2026, scheduled to take place from April 29 to May 3, 2026. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

A "1v5 ace clutch" is defined as a single player eliminating all five opponents in a round where they are the last surviving member of their team, and wins the round.

Only 1v5 clutch aces during the official playoff stage count. Group stage 1v5 clutch aces do not count toward a "Yes" resolution.

If BLAST Rivals Fort Worth 2026 is canceled, postponed after May 17, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or the official results or statistics from BLAST Rivals Fort Worth 2026 are not published within this timeframe, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be the official BLAST.tv livestream, match pages, and statistics (https://blast.tv/cs/tournaments), supplemented by HLTV.org round-by-round stats (https://www.hltv.org/). However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Scenarios shifting odds include an eco round collapse or pistol overclutch by an AWPer like m0NESY or s1mple, though historical playoff data shows near-zero incidence.

events.description: More markets for the Brazil Série A game, scheduled for May 2 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus views the May 2 Série A matchup between EC Vitória and Coritiba FBC as a toss-up, with near-even implied probabilities underscoring a closely contested affair at Estádio Manoel Barradas. Vitória benefits from home form but sits mid-table around 11th after mixed early results (3 wins, 1 draw, 4 losses), hampered by key injuries including defender Edu Ribeiro's long-term Achilles rupture, forward Marinho's thigh strain sidelining him until mid-April, and others like Claudinho and Lucas Braga. Coritiba holds a stronger 7th position with solid form (4 wins, 1 draw, 3 losses), though recent load management and minor knocks like Keno's absence temper momentum. Balanced head-to-head (11-12-9) and no major suspensions heighten uncertainty.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

This consistent positioning in multi-candidate fields—reflecting Flávio's consolidation of right-wing support amid Jair Bolsonaro's ineligibility—has driven trader consensus to imply a 65% probability for Flávio in second place, while pricing Lula's second-place odds at 19% given his frontrunner status. A Senate rejection of Lula's Supreme Court nominee on April 30 marks a rare political setback that could narrow the gap ahead of the October 4 first round.

In the past week, he urged Virginia voters to approve a redistricting referendum on April 21—which passed—congratulated the outcome, responded to the April 25 White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting, and critiqued a Supreme Court decision weakening Voting Rights Act protections on April 29. These social media statements align with standard ex-presidential activity via the Obama Foundation, showing no signs of escalation into major midterm endorsements, scandals, or controversies that could trigger resolution before year-end. Term limits preclude another candidacy, and absent late-breaking developments like health events or national mobilization, traders anticipate continued restraint.

markets.question: Will Mistral have the best Coding AI model at the end of April 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Krylya Sovetov Samara, languishing in 13th place in the Russian Premier League table with 21 points from 22 matches and a league-worst defensive record of 40 goals conceded, face a daunting task hosting sixth-placed Spartak Moscow on May 1 at Samara Arena. Spartak's dominance in head-to-head history—35 wins in 48 meetings, including a 2-1 home victory over Krylya earlier this season—underpins trader sentiment favoring the visitors, though Krylya's home form offers upset potential. eventMetadata.context_description: State Rep. Manny Rutinel commands 87% trader consensus to win Colorado's 8th Congressional District Democratic primary on June 30, driven by his Q1 2026 fundraising edge—$947,000 raised and $1.8 million cash-on-hand, surpassing former Rep. Shannon Bird's $566,000 raised and $1.1 million cash—and appeal to the district's 40% Latino electorate as a Yale-educated Dominican immigrant. Rising Mirra Andreeva (7.1%) reflects breakout potential, with odds emphasizing power and recent momentum over pure clay specialists.
